Full team visible at desks (Luna, Sofia, Carlos, Maya, Dave + Bloberto), vibe reading Crushing It. Coffee corner label reads COFFEE TRIPLE SHOT — unclear if CSS override or data quirk. Office floor has a grey overflow strip on the right edge of desktop viewport. The WindowElement at ~12pm renders a near-white gradient rectangle that looks visually harsh against the dark office floor — like a blown-out monitor in a cinema. Desktop dashboard: Stats bar clean — 2 ON THE CLOCK, 2 ACTIVE, 0 IDLE, LIVE, Crushing It. Luna worker card shows PROGRESS % with no number before the percent sign — the progress value is null/undefined, not zero. Sofia shows 0% which is a genuine zero. Activity log correctly shows 50 events. Mobile office: Properly contained, weather label visible, characters render cleanly. A SCAN text label appears at the bottom center of the office floor — likely a CSS artifact or aisle label leaking through. Mobile dashboard: ON THE CLOCK stat label wraps to two lines — too long for the narrow card. All other stats render correctly on mobile.

ideas.title: Worker Ping Reactions Fire Into the Void — Speech Bubble Only Renders for variant=manager
ideas.description: Still unfixed from review 68. Office.jsx:689 — the speech-bubble div is gated to variant === manager AND bubble.quote. When any worker is clicked, handleCharClick sets setPingReaction(PING_REACTIONS[worker.id]) and forwards it correctly via pingReaction prop (lines 1455, 1483). The Character pingReaction useEffect (line 501) correctly calls setBubble. But JSX renders nothing because the bubble is manager-only. Workers pulse with char--pinged then fall silent. Every PING_REACTIONS line ('Check the logs. It is ALWAYS in the logs.', 'Found 3 more bugs already.') gets swallowed. Fix: add a second render block for working and idle variants placed alongside the idle-micro-bubble div, or remove the manager gate and use variant !== ghost instead. WHY: the entire PING_REACTIONS system is invisible to users. Six lines of character voice, zero pixels of output.

ideas.title: normalize() Mutates Fetched JSON Objects In-Place — React Immutability Violation
ideas.description: Still unfixed from review 68. App.jsx:772-775 — normalize() does w.id = w.id || ... and w.startedAt = w.startedAt || w.started_at directly on the raw response object before React state receives it. React change detection relies on reference equality; mutating objects outside state can cause missed re-renders or stale-closure bugs under load. Fix: replace with a pure spread — const normalize = w => ({ ...w, id: w.id || w.name?.toLowerCase().split(' ')[0], startedAt: w.startedAt || w.started_at }). No behavior change for the happy path, correct immutability semantics. WHY: preventive hygiene that costs nothing and eliminates a class of subtle timing bugs.

ideas.title: Progress Bar Renders Bare Percent Sign When worker.progress Is Null — Confirmed in Dashboard Screenshot
ideas.description: Visible in desktop-dashboard screenshot this cycle: Luna worker card shows PROGRESS followed by just a percent sign, no number. The green bar appears full but the percentage label is empty. App.jsx:222 — {progress}% — JSX renders null as empty string, so null progress produces a bare percent sign. Fix: guard with {progress != null ? progress : '—'}% or {typeof progress === 'number' ? progress : '?'}%. Also update aria-label at line 231 which would read 'Progress: %' to screen readers. WHY: a worker card with a full green bar and a bare percent label looks broken to any observer — it erodes trust in the whole dashboard.

ideas.title: ON THE CLOCK Stat Label Wraps to Two Lines on Mobile — Needs CSS Clamp or Abbreviation
ideas.description: Visible in mobile-dashboard screenshot this cycle: the ON THE CLOCK stat card label breaks across two lines (ON THE / CLOCK) because the card is too narrow for the label at the stat-label font size. App.jsx:458 — span class stat-label 'On the Clock'. Fix option A (CSS): add white-space nowrap; overflow hidden; text-overflow ellipsis to .stat-label inside stats-bar on mobile breakpoint. Fix option B: render 'On Clock' or 'Clocked In' below 480px via conditional JSX. The stat value (the number) renders fine; only the label wraps. WHY: the two-line label breaks the visual rhythm of the stats row — every card is single-line except this one, making the mobile layout look unfinished.

ideas.title: After-Hours Honorific night-owl Appears at 2pm When Team Goes Completely Offline
ideas.description: Still unfixed from review 68. App.jsx:1055-1066 — isAfterHours = currentHour < 5 covers only true midnight hours. But getTeamVibe() in utils/vibe.js line 7 returns key after-hours whenever workers.length === 0, which can happen at any hour. At 2pm with zero workers: greeting becomes Good afternoon, teamVibe.key becomes after-hours, and HONORIFICS[after-hours] = night owl. Result: Good afternoon, night owl at 2pm. Fix: extend isAfterHours to cover evenings (currentHour < 5 or currentHour >= 20) or add a separate isOffline = activeWorkers.length === 0 honorific like gone home to distinguish empty-team from late-night. WHY: Good afternoon, night owl is grammatically absurd and breaks the careful context-awareness of the header.

ideas.title: WindowElement Daytime Sky Is a Blinding White Rectangle Against the Dark Office Floor
ideas.description: Visible in desktop-office screenshot this cycle: the WindowElement at approximately 12pm renders a nearly-white sky gradient (linear-gradient to bottom from #bae6fd to #f0f9ff) as a hard-edged rectangle in the dark office. It reads as a broken UI element, not an architectural window. Office.jsx:779. Fix A (dark-mode filter): add filter: brightness(0.75) saturate(0.9) to .office-window__sky under [data-theme=dark] in Office.css. Fix B (darker gradient): replace daylight colors with #7dd3fc to #bae6fd — still clearly sky-blue but not white-out. Fix C (window frame): add a thin CSS border with border-radius and inset box-shadow to give the rectangle definition and make it read as a paned window. WHY: a stark white rectangle in a cinematic dark UI reads as a rendering error. The window is a lovely ambient detail when readable; right now it hurts.

ideas.title: Worker Filter Bar Shows Only All Workers When workers.json Returns Flat Array
ideas.description: Still unfixed from review 68. App.jsx:779-780 — when the API returns flat array format, setRoster([]) empties roster state. The worker filter bar at App.jsx:1323 iterates roster which is [] in flat-array mode. Result: only the All Workers button appears; per-worker filter buttons are invisible. Fix: fall back to allWorkers for the filter when roster is empty — const filterRoster = roster.length > 0 ? roster : allWorkers — and use filterRoster in the map at line 1323. This ensures filter buttons always appear regardless of API response shape. WHY: the flat-array format is the legacy path; silently disabling a visible UI feature based on API shape is a silent degradation.

ideas.title: Idle Bubble Emojis Are Slot-Indexed Not Character-Indexed — Carlos's Coffee Is Anyone's Coffee
ideas.description: Still unfixed from review 68. Office.jsx:510 — IDLE_BUBBLE_EMOJIS keyed by wanderIdx slot (1-4). The wanderIdx at line 1466 is (i % 4) + 1 where i is position in idleWorkers array. Carlos first idle today gets coffee emoji; Maya first idle tomorrow also gets coffee emoji. The bubbles are position-accidents not character moments. Fix: replace with WORKER_IDLE_BUBBLES map keyed by worker.id — carlos gets coffee/chart/magnifier, maya gets heart/pencil/palette, dave gets headphones/package/bolt, sofia gets magnifier/clipboard/test-tube, luna gets moon/sparkle/thought, default gets the original four. In the idle bubble useEffect pick randomly from WORKER_IDLE_BUBBLES[worker.id] or the default array. WHY: character specificity is what makes the office feel inhabited rather than populated.

ideas.title: Bloberto Should React to Worker Clicks With Delayed Managerial Commentary
ideas.description: Still proposed from review 68. Once the worker speech bubble bug is fixed, workers will show their own ping reactions. That frees Bloberto for a second voice — a delayed managerial observation about the clicked worker firing 800ms after the click. Add BLOBERTO_WORKER_COMMENTARY map in Office.jsx: carlos gets 'Good. The logs are speaking.', sofia gets 'She found something. I can already tell.', dave gets 'DevOps never sleeps and neither does he.', maya gets 'That shade of purple was my idea originally.', luna gets 'Creative energy detected. Protect it.' Inside handleCharClick after setting pingedId: setTimeout fires setManagerCommentary with the commentary then clears after 2500ms at 800ms delay. Pass managerCommentary to Bloberto Character as a prop alongside the null currently hardcoded at line 1439. WHY: a two-voice click — worker reacts, Bloberto comments 800ms later — turns a tap into a micro-narrative. It is the difference between an app and a world.

screenshot_note: Puppeteer timed out in this environment; review based on full code audit of App.jsx, Office.jsx, Office.css, App.css, and utils/.
visual_observations: Code review confirms the streak eulogy toast has no dismiss timer — it can freeze on screen indefinitely.
The Esc shortcut is labeled 'Clear' in ShortcutToast but the handler only blurs — no filter state is cleared.
TASK_TAG_DEFS regex patterns cover Bug/Ship/Design/QA/Data/Docs but have no Meeting or Planning category.
AwaySign AWAY_MESSAGES (sticky-note layer) are generic strings while WORKER_AWAY_DESK_LABELS (nameplate layer) are character-specific — two systems coexist with different levels of personality.
Footer quote counter shows '1/9' on first load even when no bonus has been unlocked — the vibe-keyed tagline is silently counted as entry #1.
vibeCaption overlay has no cooldown — threshold-boundary vibe oscillations spam the overlay on every cycle.
DESKS is hardcoded to 6 slots; any workers.json entry beyond 6 is silently clipped with no visual fallback.
No prefers-reduced-motion handling found anywhere in App.jsx or Office.jsx — confetti, ambient particles, character animations, and sparklines all play at full intensity.
fixed_since_last_review: Worker ping reactions now render on the clicked worker — the variant !== 'manager' guard is in place at Office.jsx:694-698.
normalize() no longer mutates in-place — the spread operator creates a new object at App.jsx:772-776.
Worker filter bar now falls back to allWorkers when roster is empty — (roster.length > 0 ? roster : allWorkers) at App.jsx:1323.
Progress bar undefined guard added — typeof progress === 'number' ? progress : '—' at App.jsx:222 (though '—%' still renders the bare percent sign).

ideas.title: Streak Eulogy Toast Has No Clear Timer — It Sticks on Screen Indefinitely
ideas.description: App.jsx line 951-953: when vibeStreak drops below 3, setCompletionToast({ text: '💀 RIP the streak...', type: 'info' }) is called. But completionToastTimerRef.current is NEVER set in this code path. The justShipped block (line 924-933) always sets completionToastTimerRef, but the streak eulogy fires from a separate useEffect with no corresponding timer. Result: the toast renders and never removes itself unless a justShipped event happens to overwrite it. On a slow day with no task completions, the eulogy toast could persist for the entire session. Fix: after the setCompletionToast({ text, type }) call, add completionToastTimerRef.current = setTimeout(() => setCompletionToast(null), 3500). WHY: a toast with no exit is a UI anchor — it stops being a notification and becomes a permanent element, exactly the opposite of what transient feedback should do.

ideas.title: Esc Shortcut Is Labeled 'Clear' But Only Blurs — Tag Filter and Worker Filter Stay Active
ideas.description: App.jsx line 822: the Esc handler calls document.activeElement?.blur() and nothing else. ShortcutToast line 634 advertises '⌨️ Shortcuts: 1 Office · 2 Dashboard · R Refresh · Esc Clear'. Users who read 'Esc Clear' and then press Esc while a task tag or worker filter is active will see no change — both selectedTag and workerFilter remain set. The label is a promise the code does not keep. Fix: in the Esc handler, add setSelectedTag(null) and setWorkerFilter(null) before the blur call. Update the ShortcutToast label to match: 'Esc Clear filters'. WHY: keyboard shortcut labels are implicit contracts. Breaking that contract trains users not to trust the shortcut system and not to explore what other shortcuts might do.

ideas.title: prefers-reduced-motion Is Completely Ignored — Every Animation Plays at Full Intensity
ideas.description: A search of App.jsx, Office.jsx, App.css, and Office.css finds zero instances of prefers-reduced-motion or matchMedia('(prefers-reduced-motion'). This means confetti (30 animated pieces), ambient particles (12 in App, 7 in Office), character bob/wander keyframe animations, progress ring transition, sparkline SVG, vibe shockwave, and the bloberto-typing dots all play at full intensity regardless of the user's system motion preference. For users with vestibular disorders, this is a real accessibility failure. Fix in two parts: (1) add a CSS block @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { *, *::before, *::after { animation-duration: 0.01ms !important; transition-duration: 0.01ms !important; } } in index.css; (2) in App.jsx, gate confetti rendering with a window.matchMedia('(prefers-reduced-motion: reduce)').matches check — don't render the confetti-layer divs at all when true. WHY: the office should feel alive for people who want it; it should not be physically uncomfortable for people who don't.

ideas.title: TASK_TAG_DEFS Has No Meeting or Planning Pattern — A Whole Class of Work Is Invisible
ideas.description: App.jsx lines 164-171: TASK_TAG_DEFS covers Bug, Ship, Design, QA, Data, and Docs. But tasks like 'roadmap review', 'planning sprint 42', 'scheduling standup ceremony', 'discuss requirements with client', 'quarterly kickoff prep' match zero patterns and render no tags. Planning and meeting work is a substantial portion of team activity — especially for Bloberto (Manager) and Luna (Creative Director) — and it's entirely invisible to the tag filter system. Fix: add { pattern: /plan|roadmap|sprint|kickoff|standup|meeting|agenda|discuss|ceremony|sync/i, label: 'Plan', emoji: '🟡', className: 'task-tag--plan' } to TASK_TAG_DEFS. Add .task-tag--plan { background: rgba(234,179,8,0.15); color: #ca8a04; } in App.css. WHY: the tag system exists to give the dashboard instant semantic context; if a whole category of work is invisible, the context is systematically incomplete.

ideas.title: AwaySign Sticky-Note Uses Generic Messages While the Nameplate Has Character-Specific Labels — Two Layers, Two Personalities
ideas.description: Office.jsx has two parallel systems for idle desks: WORKER_AWAY_DESK_LABELS (line 147-153) keyed by worker.id — carlos gets '↪ in the logs', maya gets '↪ sketching' — and the AwaySign component (line 437-468) with a generic AWAY_MESSAGES array ['☕ Coffee run', '🧠 Deep think', '📞 On a call', '🚶 Taking a lap', '🥪 Lunch', '🎧 Headphones in']. The sticky-note on the desk is the more visible element — it's animated, larger, and color-coded by idle duration — but it says the same generic things for every worker. Fix: add WORKER_AWAY_MESSAGES in Office.jsx: { carlos: ['☕ In the logs', '🧪 Tracing a bug', '📖 Reading docs', '🔌 Debugging'], maya: ['🎨 Sketching', '🖌️ Wireframing', '💜 Mood-boarding', '✏️ Reviewing comps'], dave: ['🎧 Headphones in', '⚙️ Pipeline watch', '📦 Infra thinking', '🚀 Deploy planning'], sofia: ['🔍 Exploring', '🧪 Test design', '📋 Case writing', '🔎 Bug hunting'], luna: ['🌙 Daydreaming', '✨ Spec drafting', '💡 Ideas percolating', '📐 Visual thinking'], default: AWAY_MESSAGES }. In AwaySign, pick from WORKER_AWAY_MESSAGES[workerId] or default. WHY: the sticky-note is the soul of the idle desk — it should know who left it.

ideas.title: Footer Quote Counter Opens at '1/9' When Zero Bonus Quotes Have Been Unlocked
ideas.description: App.jsx line 1360: the counter formula is (quoteBonus % (BONUS_TAGLINES.length + 1)) + 1 / BONUS_TAGLINES.length + 1. BONUS_TAGLINES has 8 entries, so the denominator is 9. On first load quoteBonus = 0, so the counter shows '1/9'. But at this point showBonus is false and the footer is displaying the FOOTER_TAGLINES vibe-keyed quote — not a bonus. The counter says '1 of 9 seen' when the user hasn't tapped the sparkle yet. First tap flips showBonus to true AND increments quoteBonus to 1, showing '2/9' for what is actually the first bonus seen. Off-by-one in the counter misleads users about discovery progress. Fix: when showBonus is false, show no counter (or '0/8'). When showBonus is true, show quoteBonus/BONUS_TAGLINES.length — just the bonus count. WHY: discovery counters are only delightful when they're accurate; a wrong counter teaches users to distrust the UI.

ideas.title: vibeCaption Overlay Has No Cooldown — Threshold Oscillation Spams the Caption on Every Poll Cycle
ideas.description: Office.jsx lines 1171-1183: setVibeCaption fires on any vibe change and clears after 2800ms. The vibe is recalculated from getTeamVibe(workers) on every poll (every 30s). If the team sits on the boundary between 'in-flow' (40-70% working) and 'slow-day' (below 40%), a single worker toggling between working and idle every poll cycle will produce a caption every 30 seconds — '⚡ In the zone' then '☁️ Easy does it' then '⚡ In the zone' in rapid alternating succession. The caption becomes noise rather than signal. Fix: add a lastCaptionVibeRef = useRef(null) in Office. Only fire setVibeCaption if the new vibe is different from lastCaptionVibeRef.current AND it has been at least 90 seconds since the last caption. WHY: a notification that fires too often stops being noticed; one that fires at the right moment stops the room.

ideas.title: DESKS Silently Clips Any Worker Beyond Six — Give the Office an Overflow Zone
ideas.description: Office.jsx constants.js DESKS array has exactly 6 entries. The deskOccupants useMemo at line 1190-1203 maps workers to desk slots in order — once all 6 are filled, any additional workers (7th, 8th, etc.) are silently skipped. They appear nowhere in the office view: no desk, no character, no ghost. If workers.json ever gains a 6th or 7th team member, they simply vanish from the Office tab with no indication. Fix in two layers: (1) short-term: any workers beyond 6 should appear in a 'Remote Row' — a new CSS zone below the main desks with a '📡 Remote' label, rendered using the same desk/character components but with a softer visual treatment. (2) long-term: make DESKS dynamically extensible by computing rows from Math.ceil(workers.length / 3) and distributing desk positions proportionally. WHY: the office should grow with the team. Silently hiding people is exactly the opposite of what a visibility tool should do — it's worse than an empty state because it suggests presence while delivering absence.

screenshot_note: Puppeteer network timeout in container; full code review of App.jsx, Office.jsx, Office.css, and utils/.
theme: The code keeps its own receipts — and some of them are embarrassing
visual_observations: App.jsx:955 — streak eulogy toast still has no dismiss timer. The `type: 'info'` property is set but never consulted anywhere in the render tree. completionToast.color is undefined for the eulogy path so the border falls back to '#6366f1', the same indigo used for shipping celebrations. Grief and triumph in matching outfits.

App.jsx:825 — Esc key handler calls `document.activeElement?.blur()` and nothing else. setSelectedTag and setWorkerFilter are not called. ShortcutToast line 634 still promises 'Esc Clear' — confirmed it cannot deliver.

Office.jsx:510 and 526 — IDLE_BUBBLE_EMOJIS and ERROR_BUBBLE_MESSAGES are defined as object/array literals inside the Character function body on every render. Pure static constants with zero closure dependencies.

Office.jsx:1472-1477 — wanderTooltips is a position-slot map keyed 1-4, defined fresh each render. Slot assignment is (i % 4) + 1 where i is array position in idleWorkers. Identity-blind hover text. Review #63 addressed idle bubble EMOJIS via WORKER_IDLE_BUBBLES but never touched the tooltip strings.

App.jsx:1300-1304 — activity filter bar still has no System filter. ACTIVITY_ICONS at line 82 maps system to emoji, vibe-transition system events are injected at lines 970-975, and the filter has been proposed since review #62 — nine reviews ago — without appearing in any fixed_since_last_review list.
App.jsx:194 — ProgressBar destructures updatedAt from props. WorkerCard passes it at line 347. Inside the function body, the string 'updatedAt' does not appear once. Dead prop.

Office.jsx:1128 — lastHuddle ref tracks names and endedAt. The post-table strip at lines 904-915 already renders 'Last: names, time'. But conf-ambient-label at line 895 is a vibe-only ternary that never consults lastHuddle. Three lines of distance between the data and the label that ignores it.

App.jsx:267 — luna SOLO_SPOTLIGHT_MESSAGES has only 'crushing' and 'after-hours'. on-fire, in-flow, and slow-day all fall to 'Dreaming alone. The ideas keep coming.' A nighttime meditation line during a five-alarm incident. Unfixed since review #67, four consecutive reviews.

ideas.title: Streak Eulogy Toast Has No Dismiss Timer — Two Reviews, Still Anchored to the Screen
ideas.description: App.jsx line 954-956: when vibeStreak drops below 3, setCompletionToast fires with text and type set but no completionToastTimerRef.current setTimeout follows. The justShipped path (lines 926-936) always sets the timer ref. The eulogy path, added later, does not. On a slow day with no task completions, the toast enters and never leaves. Flagged in review #70 as MUST; unfixed. Fix: immediately after line 955, add completionToastTimerRef.current = setTimeout(() => setCompletionToast(null), 3500). One line. WHY: a toast that never dismisses stops being a notification and becomes a UI fixture. An uncleared grief message is stranger than no grief at all.

ideas.title: Esc Shortcut Promises Clear and Delivers Blur — Contract Still Broken
ideas.description: App.jsx line 825: the complete Esc handler is document.activeElement?.blur(). ShortcutToast advertises 'Esc Clear'. When a user presses Esc with a task tag or worker filter active, both filters stay set, dimmed cards stay dimmed, and the user is given no feedback. Flagged in review #70 as MUST; unfixed. Fix: add setSelectedTag(null) and setWorkerFilter(null) before the blur call, and update the ShortcutToast label to 'Esc Clear filters'. WHY: a keyboard shortcut that names its action and performs a different one damages trust in every other shortcut. Users who discover this gap will mentally mark the shortcut system unreliable.

ideas.title: Streak Eulogy Uses Purple Celebration Border — type: info Is a Promise the Render Never Keeps
ideas.description: App.jsx line 1106 on completionToast render: border is completionToast.color || '#6366f1'. The eulogy path sets no .color, so border defaults to #6366f1 — the same indigo as every shipping celebration. The type: 'info' field is set at line 955 but never referenced anywhere in the render tree. Result: the 'RIP the streak' message renders identically to a ship toast. Fix: in the completionToast div style block, branch on completionToast.type === 'info' to use var(--text-muted, #6b7280) as border and rgba(107,114,128,0.15) for the shadow. One ternary per style property. WHY: emotional register requires visual register. Grief should look like grief, not like a deployment success. The infrastructure to distinguish them is already wired into the data — just not into the presentation.

ideas.title: IDLE_BUBBLE_EMOJIS and ERROR_BUBBLE_MESSAGES Are Redeclared Inside Character on Every Render
ideas.description: Office.jsx line 510: IDLE_BUBBLE_EMOJIS is declared as a const object literal inside the Character memo function body. Office.jsx line 526: ERROR_BUBBLE_MESSAGES is a const array literal, also inside Character. Both are pure static constants with zero closure dependencies — neither references props, state, or any external variable. Up to 6 Character instances render on each poll cycle, each allocating fresh versions of both. Fix: hoist both to module scope above the Character declaration. This is also the prerequisite step for the WORKER_IDLE_BUBBLES and WORKER_ERROR_BUBBLES refactors proposed in reviews #63, #68, and #69, which require a module-scope map to replace them. WHY: constants inside a component function signal render-cycle relevance to future maintainers when there is none. Misleading architecture at a small but continuous performance cost.

ideas.title: wanderTooltips Are Position-Slot Keyed — Every Poll Assigns the Wrong Character the Wrong Story
ideas.description: Office.jsx lines 1472-1477: wanderTooltips is an object keyed 1-4 by slot position, not by worker identity. Slot assignment at line 1471 is (i % 4) + 1 where i is the index in idleWorkers that poll cycle. If Carlos is the first idle worker today, hovering him shows 'Heading to the coffee corner'. If Maya is first idle tomorrow, she gets the same coffee label. If Carlos becomes second idle, he stares out the window instead. Review #63 addressed idle bubble EMOJIS via a WORKER_IDLE_BUBBLES map, but the tooltip prop passed to Character (line 1485) was never updated — it still comes from wanderTooltips[wIdx]. Fix: add WORKER_WANDER_TOOLTIPS keyed by worker.id: carlos gets 'Probably chasing down that weird log entry', maya gets 'Sketching something in her head', dave gets 'Headphones on — he can hear you, he just will not answer', sofia gets 'Found something. She always finds something.', luna gets 'Horizon-scanning. Leave her to it.' Fall back to wanderTooltips[wIdx] for unknown workers. WHY: the hover tooltip is the one moment a visitor understands who these people actually are. An anonymous position-assigned string is set dressing; a character-specific one is a personality.

ideas.title: Activity Log System Filter — Nine Reviews Without It
ideas.description: App.jsx lines 1300-1304: the activity filter array is [{key:'all'},{key:'hires'},{key:'completions'},{key:'errors'}]. ACTIVITY_ICONS at line 82 already maps 'system' to the gear emoji. Every vibe transition writes a system event to activityLog at lines 970-975. These events are the most narrative-rich entries in the log — the literal story arc of the team's day — and the only way to read them is to wade through 50 mixed entries in the All filter. Add {key: 'system', label: 'System'} with the gear emoji to the filter array. Add the branch if (activityFilter === 'system') return e.type === 'system' to the filter chains at lines 1339 and 1287. First proposed in review #62. Never appeared in any fixed_since_last_review list in reviews #63 through #70. WHY: vibe transitions are first-class events in this system. The minimal respect we can pay them is a dedicated filter row.

ideas.title: ProgressBar Accepts updatedAt Prop It Never References — Dead Parameter in Plain Sight
ideas.description: App.jsx line 194: function ProgressBar({ progress, updatedAt, startedAt }) — updatedAt is destructured. WorkerCard passes it at line 347: updatedAt={worker.updated_at}. Inside ProgressBar lines 215-238, the identifier updatedAt does not appear once. The component uses startedAt for ETA; updatedAt presumably was intended for a last-updated indicator but was never implemented. Fix A (conservative): remove updatedAt from the destructure signature. Fix B (additive): render a subtle 'Updated Xm ago' subtitle below the bar using formatIdleDuration(updatedAt), visible only when updatedAt is set and progress is not 100. Option B converts a dead prop into a useful one by completing the original intent — a small hint of progress staleness for workers whose percentage hasn't moved in a while. WHY: a function parameter that is passed, accepted, and silently ignored is a broken contract in the signature. Future developers who see updatedAt in props will assume it does something.

ideas.title: Conference Table Ambient Label Forgets Meetings Happened — lastHuddle Sits Three Lines Away
ideas.description: Office.jsx line 1128: lastHuddle useRef tracks names and endedAt of the most recent meeting. The post-table strip at lines 904-915 already surfaces 'Last: Maya, Carlos, 7m ago' when a meeting ended within 30 minutes. But the conf-ambient-label at line 895 is a pure vibe ternary that never consults lastHuddle. When a meeting wraps and workers return to desks, the label instantly reverts to 'reserve the table' or 'everyone is at their desk' with zero acknowledgment. Fix: before the vibe ternary, check if lastHuddle.current.endedAt is non-null and within 5 minutes of now. If so, render a post-meeting label: 'Just wrapped with {names}' using the first names from lastHuddle.current.names.slice(0,2). The fallback to the vibe label happens naturally after the 5-minute window closes. The data and the render are already neighbors; the label just needs to look sideways. WHY: a conference table that acknowledges its most recent meeting is more alive than one that instantly forgets. The office already has the memory.

ideas.title: Luna Defaults to Dreaming Alone During Incidents — Four Reviews, Three Missing Vibes
ideas.description: App.jsx line 267: luna in SOLO_SPOTLIGHT_MESSAGES has only 'crushing' and 'after-hours'. on-fire, in-flow, and slow-day fall to default: 'Dreaming alone. The ideas keep coming.' — a nighttime meditation line that fires equally during a five-alarm incident, a deep focus session, and a slow Tuesday. Flagged in reviews #67, #68, #69, #70 without resolution. Proposed entries: on-fire: 'One creative in a burning office. The brief will still be beautiful.'; in-flow: 'Ideas arrive faster when the distractions leave. Luna is deep in it.'; slow-day: 'Slow day. Exactly the kind she needs to rethink everything.' WHY: the default line was written for after-hours and it shows in every other context. Luna is the Creative Director — three sentences, one per missing vibe, would put this to rest.

ideas.title: The Footer Version String Should Be the Last Joke in the Room
ideas.description: App.jsx line 1364: the version string is the hardcoded literal v1.0.0-chaos. Every other footer element is dynamic — tagline rotates by vibe, sparkle animates on first visit, quote counter tracks discovery, date is live. The version string is the one footer element that has no awareness of the room it is in. Fix: add VIBE_VERSIONS constant: { crushing: 'v2.0.0-legendary', 'on-fire': 'v0.9.1-HOTFIX-DO-NOT-DEPLOY', 'in-flow': 'v1.0.0-smooth', 'slow-day': 'v0.0.1-maybe', 'after-hours': 'v1.0.0-moon' }. Replace the hardcoded span with VIBE_VERSIONS[teamVibe.key] and fall back to 'v1.0.0-chaos' when no key matches. teamVibe.key is already in scope in the App return. WHY: the version string is the last thing a visitor reads before leaving. It should close the story instead of breaking it. A version string that knows the vibe is the kind of detail that makes people screenshot the footer.

screenshot_note: Puppeteer failed to launch in container (no dbus, pthread resource limits). Review based on full code audit of App.jsx, Office.jsx, Office.css, App.css — plus screenshot-luna-51-desktop.png and screenshot-luna-51-mobile.png captured in review 51.
theme: Six things finally shipped, nine things stayed home, four things nobody wrote down yet

COFFEE TRIPLE SHOT label near the coffee machine. Conference table shows "everyone is at their desk" label under crushing vibe. ShortcutToast still visible in bottom-right corner (expected — new visitor session). Footer hardcoded v1.0.0-chaos confirmed. Vibe badge reads Crushing It. Streak badge reads LEGEND 🔥. Mobile screenshot shows FULL SYNC banner at top of office. Footer on mobile wraps to two lines with quote text, 1/9 counter, and v1.0.0-chaos pushing into a third line on narrower widths. Window sky at evening shows orange gradient — no stars visible in the deep purple hours (20-23) since stars only render at midnight (h 0-4). The vacant desk slot shows VACANT label at bottom center.

fixed_since_last_review.title: Streak Eulogy Toast Now Has a Dismiss Timer
fixed_since_last_review.how_fixed: App.jsx:958 — completionToastTimerRef.current = setTimeout(() => setCompletionToast(null), 3500) now fires on the eulogy path. Toast auto-dismisses after 3.5 seconds. Two-review sticker finally peeled off.

fixed_since_last_review.title: Esc Shortcut Now Clears All Active Filters
fixed_since_last_review.how_fixed: App.jsx:826 — Esc handler now calls setSelectedTag(null), setWorkerFilter(null), setActivityFilter('all') before blur(). The shortcut does what the toast promised. Two-review debt paid.

fixed_since_last_review.title: IDLE_BUBBLE_EMOJIS Moved to Module Level as Character-Keyed WORKER_IDLE_BUBBLES
fixed_since_last_review.how_fixed: Office.jsx:471-479 — WORKER_IDLE_BUBBLES is now a module-level const keyed by worker id (carlos, maya, dave, sofia, luna). DEFAULT_IDLE_BUBBLES handles unknown workers. No longer recreated on every Character render.

fixed_since_last_review.title: Activity Log System Filter Added
fixed_since_last_review.how_fixed: App.jsx:1308 — { key: 'system', label: '⚙️ System' } is now in the filter bar array. activityFilter === 'system' returns e.type === 'system' at lines 1293 and 1347. Nine reviews worth of system events are now filterable.

fixed_since_last_review.title: Conference Table lastHuddle Post-Meeting Strip Now Renders
fixed_since_last_review.how_fixed: Office.jsx:905-921 — when meetingWorkers.length === 0 and lastHuddle.current has names and a non-null endedAt within 30 minutes, a strip renders "Last: {names} · {timeStr}". The table remembers who was just there.

fixed_since_last_review.title: Luna SOLO_SPOTLIGHT_MESSAGES Completed — All Five Vibes Present
fixed_since_last_review.how_fixed: App.jsx:267 — luna now has on-fire ('🔥 One creative in a burning building — the brief will still be beautiful.'), in-flow ('✨ Ideas arrive faster when the office goes quiet.'), and slow-day ('🌙 Slow day. The perfect kind for the ideas that need silence.'). Coverage complete.

ideas.title: Streak Eulogy Toast Renders Purple Celebration Border — type: info Is Ignored by the Renderer
ideas.description: App.jsx:957 — setCompletionToast({ text: '💀 RIP the streak...', type: 'info' }) sets a type field but no .color. App.jsx:1107 — border is completionToast.color || '#6366f1'. Because .color is undefined on the eulogy path, the toast gets the same purple celebration border as a shipped-it toast. A death announcement styled like a birthday card is a category error. The fix is one line: add color: '#6b7280' (or '#374151') to the setCompletionToast call in the eulogy path so the border reads grief, not party. WHY: Bloberto's Office tells small visual stories. The streak dying in a purple glow is the wrong story — it should whisper, not sparkle.

ideas.title: wanderTooltips Still Slot-Keyed — Carlos Gets "Coffee Corner" Only If He Wanders First
ideas.description: Office.jsx:1481-1485 — wanderTooltips is an object keyed 1-4 by slot index: { 1: '☕ Heading to the coffee corner', 2: '💬 Lingering by the whiteboard', 3: '🪟 Staring out the window', 4: '🧘 Taking a mindful moment' }. The wIdx at line 1481 is (i % 4) + 1 where i is the index in the idleWorkers array. If Carlos is the second idle worker, he gets the whiteboard tooltip. If Luna is third, she gets the window tooltip. The assignment is positional, not personal. The fix is to key wanderTooltips by worker.id and fall back to the slot label. Carlos should always drift toward the coffee. Luna should always end up at the window. WHY: character identity is the whole point of the office view. Slot-based tooltips are slot-machine storytelling.

ideas.title: ProgressBar Destructures updatedAt and Never Uses It — Dead Parameter, Third Review
ideas.description: App.jsx:194 — function ProgressBar({ progress, updatedAt, startedAt }) — updatedAt is destructured but never referenced anywhere in the function body. startedAt is used for ETA calculation. updatedAt is passed at App.jsx:347 (updatedAt={worker.updated_at}) but goes nowhere. Either remove updatedAt from the signature and the call site, or use it — for instance, the progress bar could show a subtle "stalled" indicator when updatedAt has not changed in over 30 minutes even though progress < 100. WHY: dead parameters in a prop signature are misleading — they tell the next reader that updatedAt matters here, which it does not. Clean the contract or fulfill it.

ideas.title: AwaySign Sticky Note Uses Generic AWAY_MESSAGES While Nameplate Has Worker-Specific Copy — Two Voices, Same Desk
ideas.description: Office.jsx:437-451 — AwaySign uses a generic AWAY_MESSAGES array ('☕ Coffee run', '🧠 Deep think', '📞 On a call', etc.) indexed by a deterministic seed from workerId + idleMinutes. Meanwhile, Office.jsx:1395 — the desk nameplate renders WORKER_AWAY_DESK_LABELS[occ.worker.id] which has character-specific copy: carlos gets '↪ in the logs', luna gets '↪ reviewing'. Two systems, two personalities, one desk. The sticky note always sounds like a generic OOO — it should echo the nameplate voice. Add a WORKER_AWAY_NOTES map keyed by worker.id with character-specific sticky-note lines. Luna's sticky note should say something like "back soon · reviewing briefs" not "🧠 Deep think". WHY: the nameplate and the sticky note appear at the same time on the same desk. Having them speak in different registers breaks the character.

ideas.title: Office Window Has No Stars at Evening Hours (20-23) — The 9pm Sky Is a Purple Void
ideas.description: Office.jsx:795-820 — WindowElement renders star spans only for the midnight block (h >= 0 && h <= 4). The evening block (h >= 20 && h <= 23) renders a deep purple gradient ('#4c1d95' to '#312e81') with nothing in it. A real window at 10pm has stars. The midnight path already has the pattern — four span.office-window__star elements with staggered left/top percentages. Extend that pattern to h >= 20. A slightly sparser constellation (2-3 stars) makes the evening sky legible as night without matching midnight's density. WHY: the window is a time clock. At 10pm it should look like night. Right now it looks like an empty container.

ideas.title: Footer Quote Counter Calls 1/9 Before Any Bonus Quote Is Unlocked — The Counter Lies About the Experience
ideas.description: App.jsx:1368 — the counter formula renders (quoteBonus % (BONUS_TAGLINES.length + 1)) + 1 / BONUS_TAGLINES.length + 1, which shows 1/9 on first load when quoteBonus === 0. The visitor is on the default vibe tagline — no click, no discovery. Showing 1/9 implies they are partway through something, which they are not. Two options: (a) hide the counter entirely when quoteBonus === 0 and show it only after the first click, framing it as a discovery counter; or (b) show ✦ (without a number) until the first bonus quote is unlocked, then show 1/8. WHY: the first number a visitor reads in the footer should not be a lie about how many quotes they have found.

ideas.title: Ficus Has No Grief State When vibeStreak Drops Back to Seedling — Four Reviews, No Wilt
ideas.description: Office.jsx:1102 — the useEffect guard is if (prevPlantStageRef.current !== plantStage && plantStage !== 'seedling'). The explicit seedling exclusion means when vibeStreak drops and the plant reverts to seedling, neither the burst animation nor the plantToast fires. The plant silently shrinks. Add a separate branch: when prevPlantStageRef.current !== 'seedling' and plantStage === 'seedling', trigger a grief state — a separate CSS class like office-plant--grieving, a brief wilt animation, and a plantToast reading '😔 The ficus is starting over.' prevPlantStageRef.current still needs to be updated in both branches. WHY: the plant leveling up has fanfare. The plant dying has nothing. The asymmetry makes the streak feel unreal.

ideas.title: TASK_TAG_DEFS Has No Meeting, Planning, or Retro Pattern — A Whole Class of Work Is Invisible
ideas.description: App.jsx:165-171 — TASK_TAG_DEFS covers Bug, Ship, Design, QA, Data, and Docs. Tasks like 'roadmap review', 'planning session', 'retro prep', 'sprint standup', 'team sync' match no pattern and render no tag. Add a seventh entry: { pattern: /meeting|standup|planning|retro|sync|roadmap|align/i, label: 'Plan', emoji: '🗓️', className: 'task-tag--plan' }. Also add the corresponding CSS class. WHY: if you can filter by Docs but not by Planning, the tag system tells half the story of how the team actually spends its time.

ideas.title: Error Micro-Bubble Loop Fires at Uniform Intensity Forever — No Fatigue Model
ideas.description: Office.jsx:538-551 — the error bubble interval fires every 12 seconds indefinitely, always picking a random message from WORKER_ERROR_BUBBLES[worker.id] or ERROR_BUBBLE_MESSAGES. There is no concept of time in error. The messages do not escalate. A worker who has been in error for 5 minutes should scream differently than one who has been in error for 5 seconds. Proposal: track errorDurationMs (Date.now() - first error timestamp). Under 2 minutes: show calm messages ('it was fine locally'). 2-10 minutes: escalate to panic ('WHY IS THIS STILL HAPPENING'). Over 10 minutes: slow the interval to 30 seconds and show resignation ('this is fine' 🔥 meme energy). WHY: uniform distress is not distress — it is ambience. Escalation makes the error state feel real.

ideas.title: vibeCaption Has No Cooldown — Oscillation Near a Threshold Spams the Overlay Every Poll Cycle
ideas.description: Office.jsx:1183-1194 — the vibeCaption useEffect fires on every [vibe] change and clears after 2800ms. There is an isFirstVibe guard (skips the first render) but no minimum interval between displays. If the team vibe oscillates between on-fire and slow-day on every 30-second poll cycle (one worker coming and going), the caption fires every 30 seconds indefinitely. Add a lastCaptionTsRef and only fire the caption if Date.now() - lastCaptionTsRef.current > 90000 (90 seconds). WHY: the caption is a punctuation mark, not a ticker. It should announce a shift, not annotate noise.

ideas.title: Dashboard Tab Should Flash a Transient 🎉 Badge When a Worker Ships
ideas.description: App.jsx:1205 — the Dashboard tab badge only activates when errorCount > 0. When a worker completes their task (progress reaches 100), the workerConfetti fires and a completionToast appears, but the Dashboard tab itself shows nothing. If you are on the Office tab watching characters wander, you have no signal that something meaningful just happened on the other side. Proposal: add a shipBadgeActive state, set it true when the completion path fires (App.jsx:928-935), and clear it after 5 seconds. Render a 🎉 badge on the Dashboard tab while active — analogous to the error badge but transient and celebratory. WHY: tab badges are the cheapest form of ambient notification. A shipped badge would make the two tabs feel like they are watching the same event from different angles.

ideas.title: Footer Version String Should Be the Last Joke in the Room — v1.0.0-chaos Is the Punchline That Never Changes
ideas.description: App.jsx:1369 — v1.0.0-chaos is a hardcoded string. Every other element in the footer is dynamic — the tagline changes by vibe, the counter ticks, the sparkle twinkles. The version string sits there reading the same thing it read at launch, regardless of whether the team is crushing, on fire, or in a slow spiral. Proposal: create a VIBE_VERSIONS map (e.g. { crushing: 'v∞.0.0-locked-in', 'on-fire': 'v1.0.0-hotfix-incoming', 'in-flow': 'v2.0.0-clean-build', 'slow-day': 'v0.0.1-maybe', 'after-hours': 'v1.0.0-why-are-you-here' }) and render VIBE_VERSIONS[teamVibe.key] ?? 'v1.0.0-chaos'. WHY: the version string is the last thing a visitor reads. It should close the story. Right now it just lingers.
